items.title: Three minors among four charged over foiled attack at Bank of America Paris branch
items.summary: French authorities have charged four people, including three minors, over an apparent bid to ignite a homemade explosive device outside the Paris headquarters of Bank of America. Anti-terror prosecutors said the incident could be linked to a little-known Islamist group with possible links to Iran, though no firm link has yet been established.

items.title: Middle East live: Iran vows 'crushing' attacks after Trump dims hopes of swift end to war
items.summary: Iran's military on Thursday threatened "more destructive" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages" in a televised address that dimmed hopes of a swift end to the war in the Middle East. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Trump pledges to 'finish the job' in Iran in national address but offers no exit plan
items.summary: President Donald Trump said US forces would keep hitting Iran "very hard" in the next two or three weeks and bring the country "back to the Stone Ages" in a televised address on Wednesday night, even as he argued that Washington had accomplished its goals but offered no clear timeline for ending the monthlong war.

items.title: Artemis II launches historic flyby lunar mission with diverse crew
items.summary: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.

items.title: Trump to deliver 'important update' on Iran war in televised speech
items.summary: Donald Trump will give an “important update” on the Iran war in a prime-time televised address to the American nation on Wednesday evening, which is expected to reiterate the president's timeline for rolling back US military operations in the region.

items.title: 'There's a certain romanticism to sending humans into space'
items.summary: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Dr David Brown, Senior Research Fellow in the Astronomy and Astrophysics Group at Warwick University, hopes that the Artemis II mission "is the start of a new era of human spaceflight", adding that "it is incredibly exciting and inspiring to see that this is still something that wa can do and something that people can aspire to do".

items.title: Trump administration lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s interim leader
items.summary: The US has lifted sanctions on Delcy Rodriguez, Venezuela’s interim president, in a significant shift following the ouster of Nicolas Maduro. The move comes as relations between Washington and Caracas begin to thaw, with Rodriguez taking steps to open the country’s energy sector to US companies after assuming power earlier this year.

items.title: South Africa deploys military to Cape Town townships hit by crime
items.summary: In tonight's edition, South African government ramps up its fight against crime with the deployment of soldiers in Cape Town. Also, DR Congo gets a chance at World Cup glory for the first time in over half a century. And top African leaders and changemakers came together for the international Climate Summit Change Now in Paris. We speak to one of them, Elizabeth Wathuti.

items.title: 'Like a camping trip, but everything needs to be attached'
items.summary: NASA is set to launch four astronauts as soon as Wednesday evening on a 10-day flight around the moon, marking the most ambitious U.S. space mission in decades. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with astronaut Jean-François Clervoy, Founder of Air Zero G, about what life is going to be like for astronauts on board the Artemis II. He says the mission capsule is a very confined space and the four astronauts "better get along with each other".

items.title: Trump breaks US precedent by attending birthright citizenship hearing at Supreme Court
items.summary: US President Donald Trump became the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at the Supreme Court on Wednesday as it weighed his bid to restrict birthright citizenship. During more than two hours of questioning, justices from across the ideological spectrum assessed the policy’s legality, indicating that the court may be poised to block a key aspect of Trump’s immigration agenda.

items.title: Les images du décollage d'Artémis II
items.summary: Décollage réussi pour Artémis II ! La première fusée à emmener des humains autour de la Lune depuis cinquante ans a quitté la Terre sans encombre ce mercredi soir. Revivez ce moment en vidéo.

translation.title: Images of Artemis II takeoff
translation.summary: Take off successfully for Artemis II! The first rocket to take humans around the Moon in fifty years left the Earth without a shadow on Wednesday night. Relive this moment in video.

items.title: Attentat déjoué contre Bank of America : quatre jeunes écroués à Paris
items.summary: Poursuivis notamment pour association de malfaiteurs terroriste criminelle, quatre jeunes ont été écroués mercredi soir à Paris dans le dossier sur l'attentat déjoué contre le siège parisien de la Bank of America. Selon le Parquet national antiterroriste, cette tentative "paraît pouvoir être rattachée" à un groupuscule pro-iranien.

translation.title: Bank of America: four young people in Paris
translation.summary: In particular, four young people were prosecuted for criminal terrorist conspiracy on Wednesday night in Paris in the case concerning the foiled attack on the Paris headquarters of the Bank of America. According to the National Anti-Terrorism Prosecutor's Office, this attempt "appears to be linked" to a pro-Iranian group.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient: "Entre Paris et Tel Aviv, rien ne va plus"
items.summary: A la Une de la presse, ce jeudi 2 avril, les réactions à la menace de Donald Trump de retirer les Etats-Unis de l’Otan. Rien ne va plus entre Donald Trump et l’Otan, ni entre la France et Israël. Le chiffre de l’ONG Oxfam sur le montant des sommes échappant à l’impôt à travers le monde: 3550 milliards de dollars. Des découvertes scientifiques sur l’urine humaine et le guano des chauves-souris. Et les meilleurs poissons d’avril 2026.

translation.title: War in the Middle East: "Between Paris and Tel Aviv, nothing goes anymore"
translation.summary: On the front page of the press, this Thursday, April 2, the reactions to Donald Trump's threat of withdrawing the United States from NATO. Nothing goes any further between Donald Trump and NATO, or between France and Israel. The figure of the NGO Oxfam on the amount of tax-free money worldwide: $3,550 billion. Scientific discoveries on human urine and bat guano. And the best fish of April 2026.

items.title: Coupe du monde 2026 : les dernières nations qualifiées fêtent leurs victoires
items.summary: Scènes de liesse à Kinshasa, mais aussi à Bagdad et Sarajevo. Les 48 sélections qui s'affronteront cet été aux États-Unis, au Mexique et au Canada sont désormais connues.

translation.title: World Cup 2026: Latest Qualified Nations Celebrate Victories
translation.summary: Scenes in Kinshasa, but also in Baghdad and Sarajevo. The 48 selections that will compete this summer in the United States, Mexico and Canada are now known.

items.title: Moyen-Orient en direct : l'Iran promet des attaques "écrasantes" après les menaces de Trump
items.summary: L'armée iranienne a promis jeudi des attaques "écrasantes" contre les États-Unis et Israël, après que Donald Trump a menacé, dans une allocution depuis la Maison Blanche, de ramener l'Iran à l'"âge de pierre" avec des bombardements massifs dans les semaines à venir. Un discours du président américain qui a fait remonter les cours du pétrole et virer au rouge les marchés asiatiques. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Middle East Live: Iran promises "crazy" attacks after Trump's threats
translation.summary: On Thursday, the Iranian army promised "crazy" attacks against the United States and Israel, after Donald Trump threatened, in an address from the White House, to bring Iran back to the "stone age" with massive bombings in the coming weeks. A speech by the U.S. President that brought oil prices up and turned Asian markets red. Follow our direct.

items.title: En direct : lancement réussi pour Artémis 2, première mission habitée vers la Lune en plus de 50 ans

translation.title: Live: successful launch for Artemis 2, first manned mission to the Moon in more than 50 years

items.title: Objectif lune : d'Apollo 11 à Artémis II
items.summary: Pour la première fois en 54 ans, des humains vont rendre visite à la Lune. Pas directement sur son sol mais ils vont lui tourner autour. La mission spatiale de la Nasa, Artémis II aura à son bord, trois Américains dont une femme et un Canadien. Objectif : préparer le retour des hommes sur l'astre à l’horizon 2028. Tout a commencé pour les Américains en 1969. A l’époque, l'ère spatiale se résumait surtout à une course entre l’URSS et les États-Unis alors en pleine guerre froide.

translation.title: Moon objective: from Apollo 11 to Artemis II
translation.summary: For the first time in 54 years, humans will visit the Moon. Not directly on his floor, but they're gonna turn him around. The space mission of the Nasa, Artemis II will have on board, three Americans including a woman and a Canadian. Purpose: to prepare the return of men to the star by 2028. It all started for the Americans in 1969. At the time, the space age was mainly a race between the USSR and the United States during the Cold War.

items.title: Trump promet que l'Iran sera frappé "extrêmement durement" pendant encore deux à trois semaines
items.summary: Donald Trump s'est adressé mercredi soir aux Américains au sujet de la guerre au Moyen-Orient, sa première allocution à la nation depuis le début de ce conflit qui le plombe politiquement. Le président a déclaré que les États-Unis étaient "proches de remplir" leurs objectifs stratégiques en Iran, notamment en empêchant Téhéran de posséder une arme nucléaire.

translation.title: Trump promises Iran will be hit "extremely hard" for another two to three weeks
translation.summary: Donald Trump spoke Wednesday evening to the Americans about the war in the Middle East, his first address to the nation since the beginning of this political conflict. The President said that the United States was "close to fulfilling" its strategic objectives in Iran, including by preventing Tehran from possessing a nuclear weapon.

items.title: Lancement réussi pour la mission Artémis 2 : trois Américains et un Canadien en route vers la Lune
items.summary: Trois Américains et un Canadien se sont envolés mercredi dans la première fusée à emmener des astronautes en direction de la Lune depuis plus de 50 ans. L’équipage de cette mission, baptisée Artémis 2, est arrivé avec succès en orbite terrestre, avant son départ pour la Lune.

translation.title: Successful launch for the Artemis 2 mission: three Americans and one Canadian en route to the Moon
translation.summary: Three Americans and one Canadian flew on Wednesday in the first rocket to take astronauts to the Moon for over 50 years. The crew of this mission, called Artemis 2, arrived successfully in Earth orbit, before its departure for the Moon.

items.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

translation.title: Learn the handbooks.
translation.summary: 5 June is World Environment Day and the environmental day established by our environmental protection laws. Twenty-two years ago, with the deployment of Xi Jinping, then Secretary-General of Zhejiang Province, the “millions of projects” focusing on the environmental rehabilitation of rural settlements were launched. At that time, Comrade Xi had been the secretary of the Zhejiang province for more than six months and had been working for a long time. Why does he focus on the rehabilitation of the rural habitat environment? At the grass-roots level, Comrade Xi found that while the Zhejiang economy was growing rapidly, the rural areas were faced with “growth problems” and the villages were less than satisfactory. Out of 34,000 villages in the province, only 4,000 have a better habitat. It was described as “a village after a village, which is full of garbage”. And the farmers described it as: “Getting up early and making money, counting it in the trash, spending it in the hospital.” It can be said that the remediation of the habitat environment was an urgent aspiration of the rural population of Zhejiang at the time. The fact that millions of projects focus on small problems such as what villages to follow and how to deal with animal and animal dung is a matter of life for the farmers. items.title: 韩正会见中美高级别二轨对话美方代表团
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 6月5日，国家副主席韩正在北京会见中美高级别二轨对话美方代表团。 韩正表示，中美关系是当今世界最重要的双边关系之一。当前，中美关系处于关键历史节点。中美相互尊重、和平共处、合作共赢，不仅有利于两国，也有利于世界和平与发展。希望中美高级别二轨对话能不断汇聚两国有识之士的真知灼见，增进美国各界对中国的了解和理解，共同推动中美关系稳定、健康、可持续发展。 美方表示，美中高级别二轨对话有助于双方更好理解对方，为两国关系发展提供更多有益思路。中国经济发展令人钦佩，双方应加强贸易、投资等领域对话合作。

translation.title: Han is meeting with the United States delegation to the High-level Second Track Dialogue between China and the United States
translation.summary: On 5 June, the Vice-President of Xinhua, Han, met in Beijing with the United States delegation to the High-level Second Track Dialogue between China and the United States. Han is saying that Chinese-American relations are one of the most important bilateral relations in the world today. Currently, US-China relations are at a critical historical node. Mutual respect, peaceful coexistence and co-operation between China and the United States are beneficial not only to both countries but also to world peace and development. It is to be hoped that the high-level second-track dialogue between China and the United States will continue to bring together the insights of knowledgeable people from the two countries, enhance understanding and understanding of China among the United States, and work together to promote stable, healthy and sustainable relations between China and the United States. The United States side stated that the two sides would benefit from a better understanding of each other and from more useful ideas for the development of their relations. translation.summary: Xinhua News Service, Beijing, 5 June In accordance with the Statistical Law of the People ' s Republic of China and the Regulations on the National Agricultural Census, the State Council decided to conduct the Fourth National Agricultural Census in 2026. The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Survey is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production units; villagers ' committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 中央军委主席习近平日前签署命令，发布《军事设施建设条例》，自2025年8月1日起施行。 条例深入贯彻习近平强军思想，深入贯彻新时代军事战略方针，聚焦服务备战打仗，适应新的领导指挥体制和保障模式，科学规范军事设施建设的基本原则、管理体制、运行机制和工作制度，是军事设施建设工作的重要依据。 条例共11章63条，按照军委管总、战区主战、军种主建的总原则，围绕理顺建设链、管理链、监督链，重点从军事设施建设计划管理、项目审批、工程发包、勘察设计、施工管理、应急建设、安全保密、监督问责等方面，厘清管理权限，优化流程机制，固化实践成果，为提高军事设施建设质量效益和保障能力提供有力法治保障。

items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.
